verses.text: David, broken after his great sin, pleads, 'Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness,' to blot out and wash away his transgression. He appeals to mercy, not merit. The guiltiest sinner may come, for pardon rests on God's lovingkindness alone.

David, broken after his great sin, pleads, 'Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness,' to blot out and wash away his transgression. He appeals to mercy, not merit. The guiltiest sinner may come, for pardon rests on God's lovingkindness alone.
'I acknowledge my transgressions,' he says, owning that 'against thee, thee only, have I sinned.' He makes no excuse. All sin is first against God, and true repentance confesses it openly to Him.
'I acknowledge my transgressions,' he says, owning that 'against thee, thee only, have I sinned.' He makes no excuse. All sin is first against God, and true repentance confesses it openly to Him.
He traces his guilt to the root: 'I was shapen in iniquity,' while God desires 'truth in the inward parts.' Sin runs deeper than acts. The need is not reform but a heart made true within.
He traces his guilt to the root: 'I was shapen in iniquity,' while God desires 'truth in the inward parts.' Sin runs deeper than acts. The need is not reform but a heart made true within.

'Purge me with hyssop... wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow,' he prays, asking God to hide His face from his sins. He longs for full cleansing. The blood that hyssop sprinkled points to Christ, who alone makes the scarlet white.

'Purge me with hyssop... wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow,' he prays, asking God to hide His face from his sins. He longs for full cleansing. The blood that hyssop sprinkled points to Christ, who alone makes the scarlet white.

'Purge me with hyssop... wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow,' he prays, asking God to hide His face from his sins. He longs for full cleansing. The blood that hyssop sprinkled points to Christ, who alone makes the scarlet white.

'Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me,' and restore 'the joy of thy salvation.' He asks for more than pardon. The penitent seeks a new heart and the return of lost joy, which only God can give.

'Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me,' and restore 'the joy of thy salvation.' He asks for more than pardon. The penitent seeks a new heart and the return of lost joy, which only God can give.

'Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me,' and restore 'the joy of thy salvation.' He asks for more than pardon. The penitent seeks a new heart and the return of lost joy, which only God can give.

Forgiven, he vows to 'teach transgressors thy ways' and to sing of God's righteousness once delivered from bloodguilt. Mercy makes a man a witness. The pardoned sinner becomes a guide to others on the way home.

Forgiven, he vows to 'teach transgressors thy ways' and to sing of God's righteousness once delivered from bloodguilt. Mercy makes a man a witness. The pardoned sinner becomes a guide to others on the way home.

Forgiven, he vows to 'teach transgressors thy ways' and to sing of God's righteousness once delivered from bloodguilt. Mercy makes a man a witness. The pardoned sinner becomes a guide to others on the way home.

God delights not in mere sacrifice, for 'the sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart... thou wilt not despise.' The offering He wants is a humbled soul. No ritual can replace genuine sorrow for sin.

God delights not in mere sacrifice, for 'the sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart... thou wilt not despise.' The offering He wants is a humbled soul. No ritual can replace genuine sorrow for sin.

He prays God to do good to Zion, and then the right sacrifices will please Him. Personal repentance widens to the good of God's people. A renewed heart longs not only for its own pardon but for the flourishing of the Church.
